model.comment: Base model in the H4 family (alongside H4+ and H4 Ultra).

release-date.comment: Announced and made available for order April 16, 2024.

status.comment: Production and sales suspended due to Intel N-series CPU supply issues; Hardkernel anticipates CPUs becoming available again in Q2 2026 (verified 2026-04-26). Many resellers still have stock.

open-source-hardware.comment: Hardkernel publishes schematics PDFs but does not release PCB design / Gerber files under an OSHW-compatible license. Per RESEARCH.md, schematics alone do not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: Hardkernel Co., Ltd. is headquartered in Anyang, Gyeonggi-do, South Korea (design HQ; PCBs assembled in Asia).

cpu-cores.comment: 4 cores / 4 threads (Gracemont E-cores only, no Hyper-Threading).

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Single-core turbo. All-core boost reaches 2.9 GHz; 3.6 GHz is the highest stock single-core boost.

process-node.comment: Alder Lake-N is fabricated on Intel 7 (formerly 10 nm Enhanced SuperFin).

opengl-es-version.comment: Intel Gen12 (Xe-LP) Linux driver supports OpenGL ES 3.2.

vulkan-support.comment: Mesa ANV / Intel Windows driver supports Vulkan 1.3 on Gen12 UHD Graphics.

npu.comment: Alder Lake-N has no dedicated NPU.

hw-video-decode.comment: Intel Quick Sync (Gen12) on Alder Lake-N decodes H.264, HEVC/H.265 (incl. 4:2:2/4:4:4 12-bit), VP9, and AV1.

hw-video-encode.comment: Gen12 Quick Sync encodes H.264, HEVC, and VP9. AV1 encode is NOT supported on Alder Lake-N (introduced on Arc / Meteor Lake).

ram-min-gb.comment: Sold as a barebone (no RAM); 8 GB is the smallest commonly bundled SO-DIMM. Min depends on user-supplied DDR5 SO-DIMM.

ram-max-gb.comment: Up to 48 GB single-channel DDR5-4800 SO-DIMM.

microsd-slot.comment: No microSD slot — uses eMMC, M.2 NVMe, or SATA-via-USB instead.

sata.comment: Native SATA only on H4+ and H4 Ultra (4× SATA III). Base H4 has no SATA.

ethernet.comment: 1× 2.5 GbE (Intel I226-V). Dual 2.5GbE only on H4+/Ultra.

dual-ethernet.comment: Single 2.5GbE port on the base H4.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi. Hardkernel sells an optional WiFi Module 5BK accessory.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth radio. Available only via add-on USB/Wi-Fi modules.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: 2× USB 2.0 Type-A host ports on the rear; additional USB 2.0 lines available via internal headers.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: No USB-C port; power input is a 5.5/2.1 mm DC barrel jack.

poe-support.comment: No PoE on the H4 family.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.0 and DisplayPort 1.2 each support 4K@60Hz; up to three simultaneous 4K@60Hz outputs.

mipi-dsi.comment: No MIPI-DSI on x86 SBCs in the H-series.

audio-jack.comment: 1× 3.5 mm audio out and 1× 3.5 mm audio in, plus SPDIF out.

gpio-pins.comment: 24-pin peripheral expansion header (UART, I2C, GPIO, power) — not RPi-compatible.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Hardkernel uses its own 24-pin header layout; not pin-compatible with the Raspberry Pi 40-pin GPIO.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Different header pitch/layout and 120×120 mm form factor — RPi HATs do not fit.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: Header exposes 1× UART and 2× I2C (3.3V). No documented SPI on this header.

power-input.comment: Hardkernel recommends 15V/4A; 19V/7A for builds with 3.5" HDDs.

idle-power-w.comment: Hardkernel quotes 2.0–2.9W headless idle; CNX measured ~1.5W with Ethernet disconnected on the same N97 platform. Used the lower headless figure.

load-power-w.comment: Hardkernel lists 19–22W under CPU+GPU stress; CNX measured ~24W on H4+ with extra LAN/SATA. Base H4 sits closer to 19–22W.

cooling-required.comment: Ships with passive heatsink; runs fanless under typical loads. Sustained CPU stress in fanless mode reached 87°C without throttling, but Hardkernel sells an optional active fan for sustained max load.

rtc-battery-header.comment: BIOS backup battery included by default; maintains RTC and BIOS settings.

form-factor-class.comment: 120×120 mm with passive heatsink; close to but not exactly Mini-ITX (170×170) or Pico-ITX (100×72). Hardkernel-specific form factor.

weight-g.comment: Approximate, includes the bundled passive heatsink.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Standard x86 platform — boots stock mainline kernels (tested with Ubuntu 24.04 / kernel 6.x); all major peripherals (Intel I226-V, UHD Graphics, AHCI, NVMe) are mainline-supported.

official-os-image.comment: As an x86 platform, Hardkernel does not publish a board-specific OS image; users install standard Windows/Linux/BSD distros directly. Hardkernel documents Ubuntu and Batocera among supported OSes.

distros-with-images.comment: Generic x86_64 — any distro works. Hardkernel explicitly documents Ubuntu (22.04, 24.04) and Debian. Listed only those with explicit Hardkernel/community testing.

bootloader.comment: Stock firmware is AMI UEFI; 3mdeb's Dasharo (coreboot+UEFI) is officially supported as an alternative.

long-term-support.comment: Hardkernel does not publish a stated software-support window for the H-series. As an x86 board, software longevity tracks upstream kernels rather than vendor commitments.

android-support.comment: No official Android image. Hardkernel's Android build instructions cover only Rockchip-based ODROIDs; the H4 is x86 and not on the Android support list.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch MSRP $99 (barebone, no RAM/storage). Verified 2026-04-26; reseller pricing varies due to current Intel CPU supply suspension.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Single SKU at $99; H4+ ($139) and H4 Ultra ($220) are separate products with different SoCs/feature sets.

available-regions.comment: Sold from Hardkernel (Korea) and major resellers (ameriDroid US, Pollin DE, Botland PL, etc.).

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: Hardkernel has suspended production due to Intel N-series CPU supply issues; restock anticipated Q2 2026. Reseller stock is intermittent.

documentation-quality.comment: Hardkernel provides a detailed product page, a wiki (wiki.odroid.com/odroid-h4) with hardware reference, schematics PDFs, and BIOS docs. Not quite RPi-tier but well above average.

community-size.comment: ODROID has a long-running active forum and broad recognition among hobbyists; Hardkernel is one of the established second-tier vendors.

active-forum-discord.comment: forum.odroid.com is vendor-run with active daily traffic; Hardkernel staff post regularly.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Hardkernel sells official cases (Type 1/2/3), enclosures with active cooling, Wi-Fi modules, and PSUs. Smaller ecosystem than RPi but solid first-party accessory line.
